name: Patchright
maintenance_status: Actively maintained fork with synchronization risk. The driver released v1.61.1 on June 23, 2026; the Python repository released v1.61.0 on July 5, 2026; PyPI reports 1.61.2. The project runs Playwright tests after releases but explicitly says not all tests pass and upstream changes can take days to repair.
license: Apache-2.0 for the driver and Python package, permissive and compatible with inclusion in ApplyPilot's AGPL-3.0 codebase when notices are retained. The repository also carries an educational-use/no-warranty disclaimer; legal use and third-party terms remain ApplyPilot's responsibility.
pricing_model: Free open-source/self-hosted package. No subscription, token, query, browser-hour, or concurrency fee. Costs are local Chromium/Chrome resources, package/browser downloads, testing, integration, and maintenance of a downstream Playwright fork.
marginal_cost_per_attempt: [uncertain] No vendor cash charge. Incremental cost should approximate local Playwright Chromium plus any ApplyPilot model, trace, power, and fallback cost. Measure fork-specific latency and failure retries on fleet hardware.
projected_cost_per_verified_apply: [uncertain] Compute (local runtime + model + fallback + human + amortized fork maintenance) / positively verified applies. Patchright publishes no job-application completion or cost evidence, so no savings projection is defensible yet.
billing_granularity: No external metering. Local cost accrues by machine uptime, power, storage, bandwidth, and engineering time. Any proxy, hosted compute, or model service is billed independently.
model_cost: None inherent. Patchright is a Playwright fork and does not include a model or agent. ApplyPilot retains its current token/model economics.
infrastructure_cost: [uncertain] Similar to local Chromium/Chrome under Playwright: browser CPU/RAM, profiles, downloads, traces, screenshots, and video. Fork changes may affect process behavior, but no official resource benchmark is published.
human_recovery_cost: [uncertain] Headed persistent contexts support owner takeover, login, and OTP like Playwright. Profile setup, browser warnings/regressions, challenges, and ambiguous outcomes still consume operator time; measure intervention minutes by reason.
adapter_maintenance_tco: [uncertain] Initial code changes can be small because imports and APIs mirror Playwright, but production TCO includes version pinning, duplicated regression qualification, fork/upstream lag, known incompatibilities, and security review of nonstandard patches.
verified_completion_rate: [uncertain] Unknown. The project reports generic detection-site results, not ATS-stratified verified job applications. Those claims are outside this legitimate compatibility assessment and are not evidence of quality.
failure_modes: Fork lag after Playwright releases; Python/driver/browser version mismatch; known test incompatibility; console API unavailable; init-script route side effects; execution-context race; Chromium-only limitation; extension/profile launch issue; ATS widget regression; auth/challenge; unknown required field; crash; or ambiguous submit result.
crash_recovery: [uncertain] Inherited Playwright tracing and context isolation help diagnosis, but Patchright has no durable workflow resume or transactional submit recovery. ApplyPilot must checkpoint externally and quarantine crashes after submit intent.
exactly_once_submit_safety: No built-in idempotency. Preserve ApplyPilot's duplicate checks, complete-plan checkpoint, durable submit intent, exactly one authorized click, immediate response/DOM capture, and crash_unconfirmed handling.
verification_evidence_tier: [uncertain] Inherited Playwright responses, DOM, screenshots, video, and traces should support normal evidence collection, but Patchright disables the Console API and fork regressions may affect instrumentation. Prefer ATS ID/success response, then confirmation DOM, then email.
verification_latency: [uncertain] Expected near local Playwright latency, with no cloud allocation. No ATS p50/p95 or fork-overhead benchmark is published; measure launch, action, confirmation, and delayed inbox reconciliation separately.
headless_headed_parity: [uncertain] Both Chromium modes exist, but the project's recommended configuration uses headed Chrome with a persistent context. No legitimate ATS parity benchmark is published, and behavior may differ from standard Playwright because default arguments and execution contexts are patched.
python_integration: Strong. The official Python package uses sync and async Playwright-shaped APIs and is presented as an import-level replacement. Current PyPI metadata requires Python >=3.10, compatible with ApplyPilot's Python >=3.11 runtime.
playwright_cdp_reuse: Strong but not identical. Most Chromium Playwright adapters, locators, fixtures, persistent contexts, and likely connect_over_cdp flows transfer. Patchright changes driver internals, execution contexts, console behavior, and launch defaults, so current tests must run unchanged before claiming parity.
migration_effort: [uncertain] Small for a shadow import/launch experiment, medium for production. Add an isolated dependency/worker lane, exact package-driver-browser pin, regression suite, profile rules, trace comparison, security/legal review, fallback telemetry, and rollback controls.
ats_fit: [uncertain] Chromium coverage is conceptually suitable for Ashby, Greenhouse, Lever, Workable, Workday, and long-tail hosts, but there is no published ATS benchmark. Patched execution contexts and init-script routing can create host-specific differences.
deterministic_coverage: [uncertain] Expected close to current Chromium Playwright for existing adapters, except known fork divergences. Measure identical deterministic fixture pass rates and live form outcomes before routing any workload.
escape_rate: [uncertain] Unknown. Escapes include fork-only regression, unsupported Firefox/WebKit route, profile/auth failure, unknown fields, challenge/policy state, missing confirmation, and package/browser mismatch.
control_plane_overhead: Low. It retains local Playwright command/response control and adds no model loop or hosted serialization. Fork-specific routing for init scripts and isolated execution contexts may add unmeasured overhead.
observability_joinability: Good through Playwright traces, network, DOM, screenshots, and video, but console instrumentation is explicitly disabled. Persist attempt_id, Patchright package/driver/browser versions, launch args, profile ID, route, adapter version, trace ID, and fallback reason.
persistent_profiles: Strong for dedicated automation profiles. The project recommends launch_persistent_context with user_data_dir, channel='chrome', headless=False, and no viewport override. Do not point concurrent workers at one profile or assume an active default Chrome profile can be opened safely.
existing_session_attach: [uncertain] Inherited Chromium CDP attachment should work for browsers already launched with a compatible remote-debugging endpoint, but this is not the project's documented primary setup. Chrome 136+ does not honor remote-debugging switches for the default data directory, so ordinary logged-in Chrome reuse remains constrained.
login_and_otp: Comparable to headed Playwright with a persistent Chrome profile: manual takeover and ApplyPilot's inbox/OTP flow can be reused. Patchright adds no OTP relay, password vault, or owner-control plane.
extension_support: Better than Playwright's default launch flags in one narrow sense: Patchright removes the default --disable-extensions flag, and persistent Chrome contexts can load approved extensions. Actual MV3/native-messaging compatibility and packaging must still be tested.
session_data_boundary: Local by default. Cookies, resumes, screenshots, traces, and browser state stay on the fleet machine unless ApplyPilot configures proxies, cloud storage, or model calls. A downstream browser driver is privileged code, so pin hashes, review updates, and isolate profiles.
required_field_completeness: [uncertain] Patchright provides browser automation, not a complete form planner. Reuse ApplyPilot's DOM inventory, conditional-field discovery, validation reconciliation, and refusal when any required control lacks an approved answer.
answer_provenance: Neutral and compatible. The browser does not generate answers; ApplyPilot can retain profile/resume/approved-answer provenance and send only authorized values to locators.
unsupported_question_fallback: Fail closed using existing ApplyPilot policy. Capture exact question/control context, do not infer, and route to approved-answer lookup, semantic fallback, or owner review.
prompt_injection_resistance: [uncertain] Deterministic Playwright use limits exposure, but Patchright adds no domain allowlist, hostile-page tests, secret boundary, or model prompt-injection defense. Preserve ApplyPilot's navigation/tool restrictions and keep page text out of privileged instructions.
site_permission_and_terms: Use only for ordinary authorized browser compatibility. Do not use anti-detection features or claims to evade access controls, challenges, bot policies, or third-party restrictions. Comply separately with each ATS/employer's terms and documented interfaces.
irreversible_action_guard: Keep the current ApplyPilot submit guard unchanged: expected domain and form fingerprint, all required answers approved, durable intent, one click, immediate evidence capture, and no automatic retry after ambiguous outcome.
concurrency: No vendor cap; bounded by local CPU/RAM, dedicated profiles, account rules, and browser stability. Use one attempt per authenticated profile/account and qualify multi-worker behavior with exact pinned versions.
startup_latency: [uncertain] Local launch should be similar to Playwright Chrome/Chromium with no remote allocation. Persistent profile size, extensions, browser channel, and fork startup differences require p50/p95 measurement.
warm_session_reuse: Strong with a long-lived browser or dedicated persistent context. Revalidate page, account, profile lease, adapter fingerprint, and package/browser versions before reuse; never share one writable profile concurrently.
tracing_and_replay: Mostly inherited from Playwright: traces, network, DOM, screenshots, and video should be available, while console functionality is disabled. Deterministic fixture replay remains viable, but store the exact fork and browser versions because upstream traces may not reproduce identically.
deployment_modes: Local/self-hosted Python, Node.js, and community .NET packages with installed Chromium or Chrome. Containers are possible through normal Playwright patterns. No official managed cloud service is required.
browser_version_matrix: Chromium-based browsers only; Firefox and WebKit are explicitly unsupported. The project recommends Google Chrome for its preferred setup. CDP is inherited through the Chromium stack; WebDriver BiDi support is not documented as a compatibility target.
canary_stop_loss: Pin package, driver, and browser; stop on version drift, console/evidence loss, fixture regression, profile failure, or increased ambiguous outcomes. Enforce per-attempt time/step limits, no post-submit retry, host failure breaker, and immediate rollback to standard Playwright.
benchmark_design: Run the current Playwright suite unchanged first, then at least 600 matched ATS-stratified forms with standard Playwright and Patchright on the same Chrome version/profile class. Compare field recall, action correctness, latency, resource cost, auth reuse, fallback, trace completeness, and positive confirmation. Do not include access-control bypass as a success criterion.
route_funnel: Record eligible compatibility experiment -> exact versions ready -> profile acquired -> browser attached/launched -> adapter selected -> required fields inventoried -> approved plan complete -> fill/validate -> submit authorized -> one click -> positive evidence -> verified applied, fallback to standard Playwright, blocked, failed, or crash_unconfirmed.
step_context_budget: No inherent model context. Record Playwright actions, DOM/accessibility observations, network events, screenshots, trace bytes, CPU/RAM, and fallback tokens. Keep the same bounded deterministic action budget as the current route.
cache_replay_yield: [uncertain] Existing Chromium selector and form caches should often transfer, but fork execution-context differences can invalidate assumptions. Measure warm hit, wrong-match refusal, invalidation, no-model completion, and savings before enabling replay.
confidence_intervals: [uncertain] No ApplyPilot benchmark exists. Use paired outcomes and Wilson 95% intervals for completion, fallback, auth intervention, and ambiguous/duplicate results; bootstrap cost and latency deltas.
historical_bucket_impact: [uncertain] Patchright may change a narrow set of Chromium runtime outcomes, but it does not solve expired/policy, unsupported answers, or genuine auth requirements and can introduce fork regressions. Tag historical failures with a reproducible standard-Playwright compatibility cause before projecting impact.
recommendation: REJECT AS A DEFAULT; WATCH OR SHADOW-CANARY ONLY FOR A REPRODUCIBLE STANDARD-PLAYWRIGHT COMPATIBILITY DEFECT. Its main advertised differentiation is anti-detection, which is outside the permitted use case. Standard Playwright has lower maintenance and policy risk; Patchright needs measured ATS benefit large enough to justify a privileged downstream fork.
